mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals stronger household demand and expected revenue growth, so positive RSAFS momentum predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 355655.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth has nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, causing equity risk premia to rise and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 6.7 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.39 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS upward-crossing events do not produce lower mean 5-day S&P 500 returns than nontriggered periods at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, so positive INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 106.1173 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 6-month growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day S&P 500 returns across any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When payrolls expand while retail sales contract, labor costs rise faster than demand, compressing expected margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive while RSAFS 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 136167.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-positive and RSAFS-negative interaction has positive mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or insignificant downside effect with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration while the policy rate remains pinned low signals negative real-rate pressure and future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3 percent and FEDFUNDS changes less than 0.10 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 216.71 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS observations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and household balance-sheet confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 791.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downward-crossing events do not reduce average 60-day S&P 500 returns versus baseline by at least 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with positive payroll growth confirms improving labor income and lower recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change is negative and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 6.7 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint falling-UNRATE and rising-PAYEMS signal has nonpositive mean 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward testing.
